data.title: Oppenheimer Asset Management likes Utilities, lists XLU, NRG, VST and others as Buy
data.description:
With the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) marking its two-year bull market anniversary last week, Oppenheimer Asset Management believes that a run beyond three years would further support Utilities - a sector which the brokerage has rated as overweight since early August.

“Utilities and Health Care have outperformed in a year-3 bull more than any other sector. However, Utilities outperformed more often when the cycle continued beyond year-3, and Health Care outperformed more often in a year-3 top,” Oppenheimer Asset Management’s research team stated in an investor note.

As a result, the financial institution spotlighted eight stocks and one Utility focused exchange-traded fund that it classifies as Buy for Wall Street. See the names below along with each instrument’s year-to-date performance.

  • Utilities Select Sector SPDR ETF (NYSEARCA:XLU) +28.5% YTD.
  • Atmos Energy Corporation (ATO) +22.4% YTD.
  • Constellation Energy Corporation (CEG) +128.5% YTD.
  • Duke Energy Corporation (DUK) +23.2% YTD.
  • NiSource (NI) +31.1% YTD.
  • NRG Energy (NYSE:NRG) +65.2% YTD.
  • Public Service Enterprise Group (PEG) +47.1% YTD.
  • The Southern Company (SO) +32.6% YTD.

For investors that are looking to further track moves around the complete utility sector of the economy, they may choose to further analyze other utilities-based exchange-traded funds for further details. Outlined below are some popular names worth mentioning.

Utilities ETFs: Vanguard Utilities Index Fund ETF (VPU), Fidelity MSCI Utilities Index ETF (FUTY), iShares U.S. Utilities ETF (IDU), and the Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight Utilities ETF (RSPU).

data.title: Quest Diagnostics rises on back of quarterly results, revised guidance
data.description:
• Quest Diagnostics (NYSE:DGX) is up ~7% in Tuesday trading after reporting Q3 financial results following Monday's closing bell that beat on both lines.
  • The patient lab services provider's boost was also helped by changes made to its 2024 outlook. Full-year revenues now expected to be between $9.8B and $9.85B ($9.66B consensus). Prior was between $9.5B and $9.58B. Adjusted diluted EPS expected to be between $8.85 and $8.95 ($8.89 consensus). Prior was between $8.80 and $9.00.
  • Quest Diagnostics reported cash and cash equivalents of $764M at the end of the quarter (Sept. 30) vs. $686M on Dec. 31, 2023.

data.title: Spotify reportedly working with Trade Desk to launch its own ad exchange
First Solar raised to Buy at Citi, seeing benefit in almost any election outcome
data.description:
First Solar (NASDAQ:FSLR) +2.8% in Tuesday's trading as Citi upgrades shares to Buy from Neutral with a $254 price target, raised from $200, believing the company will benefit regardless of the outcome of the upcoming U.S. presidential election.

Citi's Vikram Bagri estimates First Solar (FSLR) is discounting long-term module prices of less than $0.26/W, which appears low in either political outcome, and the analyst says he is now modeling long-term module pricing of ~$0.28/W, which is in-line with current market pricing but could be higher depending on AD/CVD and the election outcomes.

At the same time, Bagri downgrades Canadian Solar (CSIQ) to Sell from Neutral with an $11 PT, cut from $19, saying higher domestic tariffs, potential strain on liquidity from capex/CVD, lower PV volumes, and lower storage margins pose downside from current levels.

The analyst also thinks a Republican win in the election likely would raise the risk of more tariffs while a Democratic win probably would raise capex at Canadian Solar (CSIQ).

Given ongoing headwinds and negative sentiment, Bagri also sees potential for sharp positive reactions to reiteration of full-year guidance by Array Technologies (ARRY) and Fluence Energy (FLNC).

data.title: Sherwin-Williams' stock slides on uneven Q3 results
data.description:
Sherwin-Williams (NYSE:SHW) fell as much as 4.7% on Tuesday after the paint and coatings company said demand is still uneven and the positive effects of lower interest rates have yet to be seen. U.S. stock indexes by comparison were little changed.

Sales of $6.16 billion for Sherwin-Williams (SHW) were in line with the average estimate among Wall Street analysts of $6.2 billion.

Earnings adjusted to exclude one-time items were $3.37 a share, missing the consensus estimate of $3.54 a share.

Its consumer brands group posted lower sales because of continued softness in the North American do-it-yourself market, the company said. Many consumers are still struggling with inflation and more debt.

Chief Executive Heidi Petz said demand varied by end market in its professional architectural coatings business, and that lower borrowing costs have yet to drive a noticeable increase in spending. She added that demand remains choppy by end market and region in its industrial businesses.

"In what remains a tough macroeconomic environment, our strategy continues to be providing our customers with differentiated solutions that make them more productive and profitable," Petz said in a statement. "We are confident the growth and efficiency investments we have made will enable us to continue outperforming the market.”

Management maintained its outlook for the year, expecting sales to be flat with the prior year or up by a low-single-digit percentage. The company still expects adjusted earnings of $11.10 to $11.40 a share for the year.

data.title: Auto stocks grind higher as GM's strong Q3, upbeat outlook fuels gains
data.description:
By managing its inventory and offering vehicles that are still in high demand, General Motors (NYSE:GM) reported a better-than-expected third quarter, delivering the beat and raise that investors were counting on to drive shares to their highest level since February 2022 and its largest intraday percentage gain since the beginning of this year.

“We believe this was a large step in the right direction as management continues to navigate the choppy waters and ultimately, was a long-awaited turnaround for the GM story,” Wedbush analyst Dan Ives said in a note on Tuesday.

The North American ICE division continued to be the company’s cash cow, but even sales in its electric vehicle division did not disappoint. The company increased its market share in EVs to just below 10%, an increase from 7.1% in Q2, outperforming its rivals in the alternative-fuel space. And the company plans on expanding its dominance further with the addition of lower-cost and longer-range pickup trucks and SUVs, including the Silverado EV with a range of 500 miles on a full charge, and Equinox and Blazer EV models, both of which will launch in early 2025.

“We believe the direction of travel for GM profitability, going forward, is the balance between sustaining the currently strong double-digit North American ICE margins and mitigating/lowering the drag from the EV and AV business units,” Morgan Stanley’s Adam Jonas said.

During the company’s earnings call with analysts, GM CFO Paul Jacobson and CEO Mary Barra both warned of the challenges the company continues to face, namely lost market share in China, lingering warranty issues, and managing inventory levels. The combination resulted in a $700M adjustment in Q3 with high labor costs largely responsible for increased warranty expenses.

And for the current quarter, GM (GM) cautions that earnings will likely be impacted by lower-than-expected ICE wholesale volumes, attributed to downtime at its pickup and SUV facilities from Hurricane Helene, and 8 fewer production days in the quarter due to the holidays.

“Any production changes will have an outsized impact on profitability,” Jacobson said, adding that a softer Q4 should not materially impact the company’s improved projections for FY24.

“GM is continuing to deliver on its goals while raising its bottom-line guidance to consistently return excess to the bottom line and on track to achieve free cash flow to shareholders and optimize returns through repurchases and heightened dividend,” Ives added.

GM’s upbeat outlook pulled shares as much as 10% higher, giving a lift to Ford (F) and Stellantis (STLA), both of which are up more than 1%.

data.title: Apollo, SoftBank discussed forming giant fund to invest in AI - report
data.description:
Apollo Global Management (NYSE:APO) and SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY) (OTCPK:SFTBF) (OTCPK:SOBKY) have held talks about creating a giant fund to invest in data centers, chip factories, and other AI projects, exploring opportunities to fund the next wave of digital development, according to a media report.

The two firms discussed creating a fund topping $20B, though the talks have cooled since the summer and may not result in a transaction, Semafor reported on Tuesday, citing people familiar with the matter.

Any potential venture would likely include Arm, the chip company majority-owned by SoftBank, and other industry partners, the people said. One person told Semafor that SoftBank also spoke with other funding sources, but the talks with Apollo (APO) were the furthest along.

SoftBank CEO Masayoshi Son has a history of making big bets on developing sectors. He is now focused on finding a deep-pocketed partner who can help build a competitor to Nvidia (NVDA), which dominates the market for high-end AI chips, according to a Bloomberg report in February.

SoftBank's (OTCPK:SFTBY) Vision Fund famously invested billions in co-working company WeWork, which eventually imploded after a failed IPO attempt.

Apollo (APO) stock nosed down 0.3% and SoftBank Group ADRs (OTCPK:SFTBY) slid 1.4% in Tuesday afternoon trading.

data.title: Immunic late-stage program for lead asset to proceed as planned
data.description:
Immunic (NASDAQ:IMUX) announced Tuesday that an independent panel has recommended that the company continue its Phase 3 ENSURE program for its lead asset vidofludimus calcium (IMU-838) without any modifications.

ENSURE's Data Monitoring Committee has reached the decision following an interim data analysis, which indicated that ENSURE hadn't met the predetermined futility criteria, the New York-based biotech said.

However, Immunic (NASDAQ:IMUX), which has rallied more than 21% over the past 12 months, lost ~11% after the announcement.

"I am particularly excited about the positive outcome of the interim analysis of our phase 3 ENSURE trials, marking the successful achievement of a critical milestone for the program," said Daniel Vitt, Immunic (IMUX) CEO.

ENSURE is designed to evaluate vidofludimus calcium across two similarly designed global Phase 3 trials, ENSURE-1 and ENSURE-2, in more than 1,000 adults with the autoimmune disorder, relapsing multiple sclerosis (RMS). ENSURE-1 and ENSURE-2 are expected to be completed in Q2 2026 and H2 2026, respectively.

data.title: SPY records inflows reversing past weekly trends, bitcoin funds see strong inflows
data.description:
The world's largest exchange-traded fund, SPDR S&P 500 Trust (NYSEARCA:SPY), saw an inflow of $9.82B for the week ending October 18, after seeing outflows for two consecutive weeks.

The broader index tracking SPDR S&P 500 Trust (SPY) saw a gain of 0.05% during last week.

A considerable $1.07B was seeing flowing to the bitcoin fund—Bitcoin ETF flows iShares Bitcoin Trust Registered (BITI). Bitcoin (BTC-USD) recorded strong gains last week helped by a rise in volatility in the run-up to the U.S. Presidential Election.

Last week's inflows/outflows:

Together, the 11 S&P 500 sector tracking ETFs recorded a net inflow of ~$657M last week, according to data from etf.com.

5 out of 11 sectors saw money flowing out of their respective sector-wise fund. Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLE) saw the highest outflow of ~$303.5M, followed by Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLV) with ~$300.4M flowing out.

The energy fund (XLE) dropped 2.62% during the same time frame, while the heathcare fund (XLV) was down 1.04%.

The highest inflow of funds last week was seen in Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLF) of $788.5M and Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLK) saw an inflow of ~$384M.

While the financial fund (XLF) gained 1.75% last week, while the technology fund (XLK) dropped 1.24%.

Breakdown of S&P 500 sector fund flows (highest to lowest):

  • Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLF) - $788.5M
  • Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLK) - $384M
  • Industrial Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLI) - $166.88M
  • Utilities Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLU) - $79.03M
  • Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLP) - $40.13M
  • Real Estate Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLRE) - $31.01M
  • Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLY) - ($37.61M)
  • Materials Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLB) - ($77.08M)
  • Communications Services Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLC) - ($113.78M)
  • Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLV) - ($300.39M)
  • Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLE) - ($303.49M)

Commodities and Bitcoin ETF fund flows:

  • U.S. Oil Fund, LP ETF (USO) - $19.44M
  • Gold SPDR Gold Shares ETF (GLD) - $936.24M
  • Silver iShares Silver Trust ETF (SLV) - $147.19M
  • Bitcoin ETF flows iShares Bitcoin Trust Registered (IBIT) - $1070.75M
  • ProShares Short Bitcoin Strategy ETF (BITI) - $13.21M

data.title: OpenAI hires first chief economist to 'better understand AI’s economic impacts'
data.description:
• OpenAI announced on Tuesday that it has hired Dr. Aaron "Ronnie" Chatterji to be the generative artificial intelligence startup's first chief economist in an effort to better understand the economic impact of AI.
  • Dr. Chatterji is currently a professor of business and public policy at Duke University professor and was part of the Biden administration, where he helped coordinate the CHIPs Act.
  • “My career has focused on studying how innovation and entrepreneurship shape our economy and society, and I am excited to apply my skills to support OpenAI’s mission to ensure that the benefits of artificial intelligence are widely distributed,” Dr. Chatterji said in a statement. “I look forward to contributing to research that helps inform how we transition to an AI-driven economy.”
  • OpenAI, which is backed by Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) and others, said it hopes Dr. Chatterji's research will help "inform efforts by policymakers, academics, and organizations around the world to maximize the benefits of AI as an economic driver in their communities, while helping them identify and prepare for challenges that come with the adoption of this powerful new technology."
  • OpenAI announced earlier this month that it raised $6.6B in new funding, valuing the company at $157B.
